caption:
new ink study — old brick buildings along Gallatin Ave. trying to get the mortar lines right for chapter 2 backgrounds.

#illustration #inking #nashville #comicart

film roll #14 from the K1000. pushed one stop, shot mostly around East Nashville at dusk. grain is everything.

#filmphotography #pentaxk1000 #35mm #nashville

cover sketch for the Dawnstone Labels summer comp. first pass, very rough.

#freelance #albumcover #illustration #sketching

pages 24–26 are inked. lettering next. slow going but it's starting to feel real.

#graphicnovel #comicart #inking #workinprogress

studio afternoon with Jake. he was running a letterpress job, I was inking. coffee was good, no one talked for three hours. peak.

#studiospace #illustration #nashville

Zine Fest prep. printing chapter 1 as a standalone — 24 pages, riso on cream stock. excited and terrified.

#zinefest #nashvillezinefest #selfpublishing #comicart

film roll #12 — Sunday morning laundromat on Eastland. nothing happening and everything happening.

#filmphotography #pentaxk1000 #bwfilm #eastNashville

practicing market stall drawings — trying to get the clutter right. lots of reference photography.

#illustration #worldbuilding #comicart #graphicnovel

holiday poster for Hillsboro Village rough draft. client wants 'warm and slightly eerie.' challenge accepted.

#freelance #posterdesign #illustration

film roll #10. Percy Warner Park in February. bare trees, no one around. these are the shots I love most.

#filmphotography #pentaxk1000 #nashville #parklife

character study — the market keeper. 4 hours of reference and 20 minutes of actual drawing. feels right.

#characterdesign #illustration #graphicnovel

Makers Collective market was good. sold 11 zines, traded a print for a handmade notebook. this is the life.

#makerscollective #nashville #zinefest #illustration

film roll #8. Copper Kettle before open. low light, tungsten pushed. Jordan let me stay before shift.

#filmphotography #coffeeshop #bwfilm #pentaxk1000

spread from chapter 3 script — this is the scene I've been building toward for two years. pencils soon.

#graphicnovel #comicart #workinprogress

album cover final for Benny @ Dawnstone. really happy with how the figure turned out.

#freelance #albumcover #illustration #indiemusic

film roll #6. Radnor Lake, golden hour. I keep coming back to this light.

#filmphotography #pentaxk1000 #nashville #nature

page 14 inked. this panel took me three tries but now it's done and I won't look at it for a week.

#comicart #inking #graphicnovel #illustration

first D&D campaign as the one who draws the party portraits. Marcus runs a good game. we're all doomed.

#dungeonsanddragons #dndart #characterdesign

Zine Fest 2025. this was the booth. sold out of the mini-print series. thank you Nashville.

#zinefest #nashvillezinefest #selfpublishing #illustration

film roll #4. bike commute to the studio. Germantown brickwork in early morning.

#filmphotography #pentaxk1000 #cycling #nashville

roughed out the first market stall in chapter 2. Southern Victorian storefront meets something older.

#graphicnovel #illustration #worldbuilding #comicart

sourdough #34 and a Sunday afternoon. Gloop is thriving.

#sourdough #sourdoughbread #sundaybaking #nashville

sketchbook dump — thumbnails for pages 1–8 of chapter 1. rough but the bones are good.

#sketchbook #thumbnails #comicart #graphicnovel

film roll #1. first roll I developed myself. disaster and magic in equal parts.

#filmphotography #homedevelopment #pentaxk1000 #analog
ink study — Chapter 2 page 32 — the market gate at 4th & Main. nights of Micron 05 for the wrought iron.
Ch.2 p.34 — first light behind the vendor stalls. tried a warmer under-hatching than usual.
Ch.2 p.36 rough — trader's hands panel. still figuring the finger anatomy in the middle tier.
p.38 spread — the market ledger. every entry a memory. this one took two nights.
Ch.2 p.39 — the memory buyer. quiet page. mostly negative space.
Ch.2 p.41 — first appearance of the ghost cartographer. hand-lettered his dialogue.
p.43 — the mortar wall scene, panel 3. slow inks tonight.
Ch.2 p.45 — night market wide shot at 2am. lanterns took forever.
Ch.3 thumbnails — the two-timeline chapter. figuring out the visual anchor.
Ch.3 p.55 rough — protagonist's kitchen. the mug across timelines.
Ch.3 p.57 — mortar wall echo. tried a lighter hatching pass.
Ch.3 p.58 — the second buyer. quiet page, kept the palette narrow.
urban sketch — Cumberland River bridge, 6:40am. moleskine + micron 03.
urban sketch — East Nashville corner store, morning light.
urban sketch — Copper Kettle bar from behind, 15 minutes before open.
urban sketch — Germantown alley, rain shadows on brick.
urban sketch — front porch of a 1920s shotgun house, Eastland Ave.
urban sketch — laundromat interior, Sunday morning quiet.
urban sketch — the Cumberland ferry landing at low sun.
urban sketch — the studio window, backlit plants.
urban sketch — Radnor Lake overlook, hazy morning.
urban sketch — sunrise from the Woodland St bridge.
Pentax K1000 — Tri-X 400. bike parked outside the studio, afternoon shadow.
Pentax K1000 — HP5+. Copper Kettle bar in low light.
Pentax K1000 — Tri-X 400. Cumberland River, foggy.
Pentax K1000 — Tri-X 400. plants on my desk, window light.
Pentax K1000 — HP5+. Jake at the printing press.
Pentax K1000 — Tri-X 400. Grandma Dot's kitchen, Franklin.
Pentax K1000 — HP5+. Radnor Lake overlook.
Pentax K1000 — Tri-X 400. laundromat, Eastland Ave.
Pentax K1000 — HP5+. rooftop, East Nashville dusk.
Pentax K1000 — Tri-X 400. the studio window, plants.
oat cortado at Copper Kettle. the pour hit right this morning.
new seasonal drink menu — illustrated it last week. see if you can spot the ghost pepper mocha.
cortado + Ethiopian Yirgacheffe. the 'window seat' rotation. floral, bright.
closing shift at Copper Kettle. slow Tuesday. drawing at the bar.
new bean rotation dropped today. cupping notes below.
bar shot after service. that's Chris behind the machine.
pour test for the new competition drink. focus + play.
morning open at Copper Kettle. the light is unreal on Gallatin at 6:45.
studio day with Jake. he's printing, I'm inking. no talking for hours. best.
studio window in Germantown, plants doing well this winter.
Jake's letterpress in action — riso zine covers for the small-press collective.
my corner of the studio. the inkstained taboret. controlled chaos.
studio brief — Ch.2 pages taped up for review.
studio Saturday. Jake's printing, coffee refills, no phone.
late studio night. two more pages to ink before I ride home.
studio window at golden hour. Jake bringing coffee from the kettle.
packing for Nashville Zine Fest! table T-42, come say hi.
Zine Fest 2026 — table set up. Ch.1 preview zine is here!
Zine Fest 2026 — mid-day at the table. the print swap is stacked this year.
Zine Fest 2026 — day's over, my hands smell like fresh ink. thank you all.
Makers Collective Oct market — position V-14. zines and prints.
Makers Collective Oct — post-market wrap. sold out of the small print.
Zine Fest volunteer setup — moving tables into Eastside Bowl.
Chapter 1 preview zine — first proof from Jake's press. hand-stitched.
Micron 01 vs 05 comparison. same subject. the 05 has more presence.
brush pen test — Sakura Pigma vs Kuretake. verdict: it depends.
ink stain on the desk, three years in. character.
Copic palette study for the fall LP cover. wet blacks + cool grays.
nib closeup — the way ink beads at the tip is somehow meditative.
supply run at Blick. Bristol pack + Micron restock + one Copic marker.
hatching study — trying to keep the strokes shorter and tighter.
thumbnails for Ch.3. eight small ideas that need to become one.
cover thumbnails for the Dawnstone fall LP. water imagery.
hand-lettering test — new alphabet for chapter titles.
Sunday loaf — Gloop is thriving. rosemary this week.
Sunday loaf — cold-proof overnight test. crumb was worth the wait.
Sunday loaf — double-hydration attempt. it worked.
Sunday loaf — sourdough rye 50/50. Derek's calling it a keeper.
Sunday loaf — with Grandma Dot's cornbread on the side.
Sunday loaf — dark bake, thicker crust.
houseplants status: monstera thriving, fiddle-leaf Gerald hanging on.
Radnor Lake hike with Jake. cold air, no phones.
Percy Warner hike. leaves finally turning.
bike commute at 6:20am, Cumberland River bridge. cold hands.
Sunday laundromat routine, Eastland Ave. brought the sketchbook.
thrift find — 1980s flannel. $4. going into rotation.
